{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Task 1: Exploring and Visualizing the Iris Dataset\n",
    "\n",
    "**Objective:** Load, inspect, and visualize the Iris dataset to understand feature distributions and relationships between species.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Dataset:** Iris dataset (loaded via `scikit-learn`, the standard offline-friendly source for this classic dataset \u2014 equivalent to the seaborn/CSV version).\n",
    "\n",
    "**Skills practiced:** data loading & inspection with pandas, descriptive statistics, and visualization with matplotlib/seaborn.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "execution_count": 1,
   "source": [
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import seaborn as sns\n",
    "from sklearn.datasets import load_iris\n",
    "\n",
    "sns.set_style(\"whitegrid\")\n",
    "\n",
    "iris_raw = load_iris()\n",
    "df = pd.DataFrame(iris_raw.data, columns=iris_raw.feature_names)\n",
    "df['species'] = pd.Categorical.from_codes(iris_raw.target, iris_raw.target_names)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Dataset loaded successfully.\")\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "stream",
     "name": "stdout",
     "text": [
      "Dataset loaded successfully.\n"
     ]
    }
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 1. Basic inspection"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "execution_count": 2,
   "source": [
    "print(\"Shape:\", df.shape)\n",
    "print(\"\\nColumns:\", list(df.columns))\n",
    "print(\"\\nFirst 5 rows:\")\n",
    "print(df.head())\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "stream",
     "name": "stdout",
     "text": [
      "Shape: (150, 5)\n",
      "\n",
      "Columns: ['sepal length (cm)', 'sepal width (cm)', 'petal length (cm)', 'petal width (cm)', 'species']\n",
      "\n",
      "First 5 rows:\n",
      "   sepal length (cm)  sepal width (cm)  ...  petal width (cm)  species\n",
      "0                5.1               3.5  ...               0.2   setosa\n",
      "1                4.9               3.0  ...               0.2   setosa\n",
      "2                4.7               3.2  ...               0.2   setosa\n",
      "3                4.6               3.1  ...               0.2   setosa\n",
      "4                5.0               3.6  ...               0.2   setosa\n",
      "\n",
      "[5 rows x 5 columns]\n"
     ]
    }
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "execution_count": 3,
   "source": [
    "print(\"--- .info() ---\")\n",
    "df.info()\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "stream",
     "name": "stdout",
     "text": [
      "--- .info() ---\n",
      "<class 'pandas.DataFrame'>\n",
      "RangeIndex: 150 entries, 0 to 149\n",
      "Data columns (total 5 columns):\n",
      " #   Column             Non-Null Count  Dtype   \n",
      "---  ------             --------------  -----   \n",
      " 0   sepal length (cm)  150 non-null    float64 \n",
      " 1   sepal width (cm)   150 non-null    float64 \n",
      " 2   petal length (cm)  150 non-null    float64 \n",
      " 3   petal width (cm)   150 non-null    float64 \n",
      " 4   species            150 non-null    category\n",
      "dtypes: category(1), float64(4)\n",
      "memory usage: 5.0 KB\n"
     ]
    }
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "execution_count": 4,
   "source": [
    "print(\"--- .describe() ---\")\n",
    "print(df.describe())\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "stream",
     "name": "stdout",
     "text": [
      "--- .describe() ---\n",
      "       sepal length (cm)  sepal width (cm)  petal length (cm)  petal width (cm)\n",
      "count         150.000000        150.000000         150.000000        150.000000\n",
      "mean            5.843333          3.057333           3.758000          1.199333\n",
      "std             0.828066          0.435866           1.765298          0.762238\n",
      "min             4.300000          2.000000           1.000000          0.100000\n",
      "25%             5.100000          2.800000           1.600000          0.300000\n",
      "50%             5.800000          3.000000           4.350000          1.300000\n",
      "75%             6.400000          3.300000           5.100000          1.800000\n",
      "max             7.900000          4.400000           6.900000          2.500000\n"
     ]
    }
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 2. Visualizations"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### 2a. Scatter plot \u2014 relationships between features"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "execution_count": 5,
   "source": [
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))\n",
    "\n",
    "sns.scatterplot(data=df, x='sepal length (cm)', y='sepal width (cm)',\n",
    "                 hue='species', ax=axes[0], s=60)\n",
    "axes[0].set_title('Sepal Length vs Sepal Width')\n",
    "\n",
    "sns.scatterplot(data=df, x='petal length (cm)', y='petal width (cm)',\n",
    "                 hue='species', ax=axes[1], s=60)\n",
    "axes[1].set_title('Petal Length vs Petal Width')\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "display_data",
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {}
    }
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### 2b. Histograms \u2014 value distributions"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "execution_count": 6,
   "source": [
    "df.drop(columns='species').hist(figsize=(10, 8), bins=20, edgecolor='black')\n",
    "plt.suptitle('Feature Distributions', y=1.02)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "display_data",
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {}
    }
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### 2c. Box plots \u2014 outlier detection per species"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "execution_count": 7,
   "source": [
    "features = iris_raw.feature_names\n",
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 9))\n",
    "for ax, feat in zip(axes.flat, features):\n",
    "    sns.boxplot(data=df, x='species', y=feat, ax=ax)\n",
    "    ax.set_title(f'{feat} by species')\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "display_data",
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {}
    }
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### 2d. Pairwise relationships (bonus)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "metadata": {},
   "execution_count": 8,
   "source": [
    "sns.pairplot(df, hue='species', diag_kind='hist')\n",
    "plt.suptitle('Pairplot of Iris Features', y=1.02)\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "display_data",
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {}
    }
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 3. Insights & Final Results\n",
    "\n",
    "- The dataset contains **150 rows, 4 numeric features, and 1 target (species)** with no missing values.\n",
    "- **Setosa** is linearly separable from the other two species on almost every feature, especially petal length/width \u2014 a single threshold on petal length (~2 cm) cleanly isolates it.\n",
    "- **Versicolor** and **Virginica** overlap somewhat on sepal measurements but separate reasonably well on petal length/width.\n",
    "- Box plots show a handful of mild outliers (e.g., in sepal width for setosa) but nothing extreme enough to warrant removal.\n",
    "- Petal length and petal width are the most discriminative features for species classification, which matches why simple models (e.g., k-NN, decision trees) achieve very high accuracy on this dataset.\n"
   ]
  }
 ],
